title: Target Sum
description: You are given an integer array nums and an integer target. You want to build an expression out of nums by adding one of the symbols '+' and '-' before each integer in nums and then concatenate all the integers. Return the number of different expressions that you can build, which evaluates to target.
thinking_questions: Each number can be + or -. What's the state space for memoized DFS?
State: (index, current_sum). How many unique states are there?
Can you reduce to a subset-sum problem? (P-N = target, P+N = total, so P = (target+total)/2)

hints.L1: Use DFS with memoization: at each index, try adding or subtracting the number, cache by (index, current_sum).
hints.L2: Memo[i][s] = number of ways to assign +/- to nums[i:] to reach remaining sum s. Or reduce to subset-sum: find subset summing to (total+target)/2.

follow_up_questions: How does the subset-sum reduction work mathematically?
Why does nums containing zeros increase the count?
Could you solve this bottom-up with a 2D dp table?
solution_notes: DFS + memo on (index, remaining_sum). O(n * total_sum) states. Subset-sum reduction: P=(total+target)/2.
